steps.title: Identify what makes an effective narrative opening
steps.content: A narrative beginning should anchor readers at the chronological and biographical starting point—establishing WHO, WHEN, and WHERE the story begins. This creates the foundation from which the narrative unfolds.

steps.title: Match the opening frame to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A explicitly frames this with “Merle Oberon's story begins” and anchors all three elements: who (Merle Oberon), where (Mumbai, India), and when (1911).

This provides the classic narrative opening. The other three either bypass the narrative framing entirely (B, C) or awkwardly reference the future before the beginning (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
